_comment: Two scan-pose sets the LLM chooses between when looking for objects. Same schema as before: position in metres (link_base frame), rpy in DEGREES. Tune these with the face UI 'Capture Pose' button — jog the arm to a good viewpoint and paste the captured entry into the right set. 'table_scan_poses': viewpoints looking down at the tabletop (for things resting on the table, e.g. a cup or a red box). 'distance_scan_poses': viewpoints looking farther out / up (for things across the room, e.g. when the user asks 'what is that over there'). The old 'search_poses' key is still honored as a fallback for 'table'.

distance_scan_poses._note: PLACEHOLDER — tune with the Capture Pose button. Aim the camera outward/up to see across the room.
PLACEHOLDER — tune with the Capture Pose button.
